title: Braya novae-angliae
taxonomy: Braya novae-angliae is classified within the family Brassicaceae. This family, also known as the mustard or cabbage family, comprises over 338 genera and approximately 4,000 species. Braya novae-angliae is a member of the genus Braya, which contains about 7 species of flowering plants. The genus Braya is further classified within the tribe Brassiceae, and then within the subfamily Brassicoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.

Braya novae-angliae has been used in traditional medicine, but there is limited scientific research on its specific medicinal uses.
